title: Seshat BI source-drift findings contract
description: The machine-readable output contract for the F014 source-drift detector (roadmap F014, spec 015, taxonomy `docs/readiness/source-drift.md`). SEAM ONLY: this schema pins the SHAPE a future detector emits; no detector runtime exists yet and none is wired to this file. It is the machine-readable counterpart of the human `templates/source-drift-report.md` and carries the SAME two firm rules: (1) findings carry the MEASURED before/after magnitude + class + status + blocking reasons, NEVER a rolled-up numeric drift 'score' (hard rule #9, Principle V); (2) grain/PK, returns-rule, PII-surface and identity-bearing semantic-pair drift are Principle-V human seams the detector MEASURES and CLASSIFIES and raises as a blocker, but NEVER re-decides. A drift comparison runs only against a committed baseline profile; absent a live re-profile the report is marked pending, never a fabricated diff.

table.description: The `<schema>.<table>` identity being compared (matches the baseline source-profile's table).

baseline.description: Repo-relative path (+ optional @commit/date) of the committed source-profile that earned Source Ready `pass` -- the baseline the observed re-profile is compared against.

findings.description: One entry per drift class that fired. A class that did not fire is omitted (not recorded as a null/zero finding). Empty array = no material drift observed against the baseline.

status.description: The resulting Source Ready status for the table AFTER this comparison. Derived from the fired classes' severities per the taxonomy status mapping -- NOT a numeric score.

blocking_reasons.description: One human-readable reason per fatal (`blocked`) finding: the class + column + measured fact. Enumerated, never rolled up. Empty unless status is `blocked`.

evidence.description: Named evidence backing this report -- typically this findings file, the baseline profile, and the observed re-profile. Provenance, never new logic.

principle_v_handoff.description: One open question per HARD-STOP (Principle-V) finding raised to a named human owner. The detector raises; the owner decides. NEVER auto-resolved here. Empty when no Principle-V class fired.

driftClass.description: The nine drift classes of the F014 taxonomy (docs/readiness/source-drift.md). The three always-Principle-V classes are grain_pk_drift, returns_rule_drift, pii_surface_drift; semantic_pair_drift is Principle-V only when it underpins identity.

severity.description: Categorical severity of a single finding -- the class default per the taxonomy, escalated where the doc allows (e.g. column_retyped -> blocked when it touches a key/measure). Categorical only; never a numeric score (hard rule #9).

status.description: Source Ready spine status. `pass` = no material drift; `warning` = only non-fatal classes fired (does NOT auto-promote); `blocked` = at least one fatal class fired; `not_started` = no baseline, so the detector did not run; `pending_live_reprofile` = no live re-profile available (no DSN / `db` extra), recorded alongside `warning`, never a fabricated comparison.

observed.description: Provenance of the observed re-profile the baseline was compared against.

available.description: Whether a live re-profile was actually obtained. false => status must be pending_live_reprofile (+ warning); findings must NOT fabricate a diff.

reprofiled_at.description: ISO date of the observed re-profile; null when unavailable. Stamped by the runtime, not baked into this schema.

reprofiled_by.description: Who/what produced the observed re-profile (analyst / agent / runtime); null when unavailable.

finding.description: A single drift-class finding. Carries the MEASURED before/after magnitude as strings (e.g. "3.1%" -> "11.7%", "TEXT" -> "numeric", "N distinct" -> "M distinct") -- numbers, not adjectives. There is deliberately NO per-finding score and NO rolled-up score field.

column.description: The column/field (or PK column set) the drift was observed on.

before.description: The MEASURED baseline value as a string (e.g. "absent", "TEXT", "3.1%", "is_unique=true"). A measurement, never an adjective.

after.description: The MEASURED observed value as a string (e.g. "present (numeric)", "11.7%", "COUNT(*) != COUNT(DISTINCT pk)"). A measurement, never an adjective.

principle_v.description: true when this finding is a Principle-V human seam (grain/PK, returns-rule, PII-surface, or identity-bearing semantic-pair) that MUST appear in principle_v_handoff and MUST NOT be re-decided by the detector.

note.description: Optional short qualifier (e.g. "escalated: touches a key", "provable rename -> mapping review only"). Never a verdict on a Principle-V seam.

handoffQuestion.description: An open question raised to a named human owner for one Principle-V finding. The detector states the measured fact and the question; it does not answer it.

question.description: The decision the human owner must make (e.g. "is the new grain acceptable, or is dedup a defect?").

measured_fact.description: The measured signal that triggered the question (e.g. a duplicate count, a missingness magnitude, a reappeared column name).

owner.description: The role that decides (e.g. analyst, governance). Named, not resolved.
